hypothesis: Fine-tuning the pre-trained eSEN model on ~100 DFT-evaluated images sampled along linear interpolations between initial and final solids while using a loss that weights stress predictions twice as strongly as forces and energies will reduce the model's stress RMSE by >50% and decrease the number of DFT SSNEB restart iterations required to converge to the VASP MEP by >30% relative to the untuned eSEN.
